status_note: US-China retaliatory ag tariffs were suspended under the October 30, 2025 Busan agreement (extended through Nov 10, 2026). SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs Feb 20, 2026; Section 122 global 10% tariff took effect Feb 24, 2026. March 2026 USTR Section 301 investigations against China and 15 other trading partners are pending (hearings April 28). Rates below reflect the post-Busan truce state — verify against USTR and Federal Register before trading decisions.
headline: Ag tariff truce with China holding post-Busan agreement; Section 301 investigations and SCOTUS-IEEPA aftermath create policy uncertainty.

rows.note: March 2025 retaliatory 10% was suspended under the Oct 30, 2025 Busan agreement. Section 301 investigation pending (Apr 28 hearing) could re-escalate.
March 2025 retaliatory 15% was suspended under the Busan agreement. China purchases remain below pre-trade-war levels; Brazil retains market share.
March 2025 retaliatory 15% suspended under Busan agreement. Minimal baseline China exposure; trade impact modest compared to soybeans/pork.
March 2025 retaliatory 10% suspended under Busan agreement. Baseline MFN rate returned. China remains a major US pork market.
USMCA-compliant. Penn Wharton data: USMCA exemption share reached 86.3% of Canada/Mexico imports by February 2026.
USMCA-compliant. Mexico remains the top corn export destination; Canadian demand steady.

EU anti-dumping duty on US undenatured ethanol in place since 2013 (€83.03/metric ton base); some member states (e.g., Netherlands under RED III) apply higher max rates up to €192/m³. UK-US trade deal (May 2025) opened 1.4B L/yr duty-free quota for UK only. Verify member-state customs for specific shipments.

timeline.event: Court of International Trade hears oral arguments on Section 122 global-tariff challenges (Oregon v. Trump, Burlap & Barrel v. Trump); ruling timeline unclear.
China's MOFCOM launches retaliatory investigations into US trade practices on 'green products' and 'supply chain disruption' in response to US Section 301 probes.
USTR initiates Section 301 investigations targeting China, EU, Mexico, and 13 other trading partners for 'excess capacity' and 'forced labor'; hearings scheduled April 28.
IEEPA tariffs (reciprocal, fentanyl, Russian oil, Brazil) terminated; Section 122 global 10% tariff takes effect under Proclamation 11012 (150 days).
US Supreme Court rules IEEPA does not authorize tariff imposition; April 2025 reciprocal tariffs voided; refund process under CBP CAPE system begins April 20.

Trump-Xi meeting in Busan, South Korea: extends US 10% tariff rate on China through Nov 10, 2026; China lifts retaliatory agricultural tariffs in exchange for US dropping additional 10% fentanyl tariff.
US-China Geneva agreement: both sides cut reciprocal tariffs from April peaks (US 145%, China 125%) to 10% each for 90 days, later extended.
China imposes first-tranche retaliatory tariffs on US ag: 15% on wheat, corn, cotton, chicken; 10% on soybeans, pork, beef, dairy (suspended under Oct 30, 2025 Busan agreement).
